mnemonics: MAR is the car that goes to the garage (memory address) to get the word. MDR is the bag you put the word in to bring it back.
keyPoints: The CPU sends the desired address to the Memory Address Register (MAR).
The memory unit performs a read operation and returns the data.
The returned data word is loaded into the Memory Data Register (MDR) for the CPU to use.
commonMistakes: Assuming the data is sent directly to a general-purpose register instead of the MDR first.
Forgetting that the address must be placed on the address bus and a read signal must be activated.
Confusing the roles of the MAR (holds address) and MDR (holds data).
